frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Arre bhai, aajkal educated bolke sab chhod dete hain, but shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra pehne to aurat suhagan lagti hai, settled lagti hai. Yeh koi zabardasti thodi hai, hamari parampara hai — apne pati ke naam se, uske liye thoda maan rakhna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as a natural and respectable duty of a married woman, tying her identity to her husband. It encodes tradition framing, completeness/settledness framing, and mild judgment of women who do not wear sindoor or mangalsutra.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
